name: adv-discover
title: ADV Discover Command
purpose: Defines the rigor requirements for /adv-discover. The discover command gathers current-state evidence, investigates edge cases, scans for conflicts and related patterns, and produces structured findings for /adv-agree. It must extend prior research rather than rehash it.

requirements.title: Discovery Checklist Enforcement
requirements.body: /adv-discover output MUST contain a Discovery Checklist section listing each mandatory protocol step with PASS or SKIP and a reason. This ensures no protocol step is silently omitted.

scenarios.title: Checklist present in discovery output
given: A /adv-discover invocation completes successfully
scenarios.when: The discovery output is persisted via adv_change_update
then: The output contains a 'Discovery Checklist' section
The checklist lists at least 7 mandatory protocol steps
Each step has a PASS or SKIP status with a reason

requirements.title: Phase 1.5 Skill Discovery Enforcement
requirements.body: /adv-discover MUST execute the skill discovery protocol from ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md and emit a Skills Considered section. This prevents the agent from skipping domain-specific skill loading.

scenarios.title: Skill discovery executes and reports results
given: Trusted skill directories exist with SKILL.md files
then: The output contains a 'Skills Considered' section
The section lists examined skills with match assessment and action taken

scenarios.title: No skills available is reported explicitly
given: No SKILL.md files exist in trusted skill directories
then: The output reports 'Skills considered: none available'
The Skills Considered section is not omitted

requirements.title: Prior Research Extension
requirements.body: When prior research artifacts exist (temp/*.md, docs/*-prep.md including /adv-improve research packs, related archived changes), /adv-discover MUST cite them in an Extends section AND add at least one new finding beyond what they contained. This prevents discovery from rehashing prior work. When a cited pack contains Competitors & Alternatives or Emerging Patterns sections relevant to an open design question, /adv-discover MUST cite those sections in the LBP Check.

scenarios.title: Prior artifacts cited and extended
given: A prior research artifact exists in temp/, docs/, or a related archived change
then: The output includes an 'Extends' section citing the artifact
The output adds at least one new finding not present in the cited artifact

scenarios.title: No prior research is non-blocking
given: No prior research artifacts exist in any canonical location
then: The output reports 'No prior research found'
Discovery does not block or fail

scenarios.title: Relevant /adv-improve research pack sections are cited in LBP Check
given: A docs/*-prep.md research pack is cited in the Extends section
The pack contains Competitors & Alternatives or Emerging Patterns sections
At least one open design question names external tools or libraries as a realistic alternative
then: The LBP Check cites the specific pack sections relevant to the open question
The LBP Check does not silently ignore the pack content

requirements.title: Gated External-Solution Check
requirements.body: When the proposal's Discovery Agenda contains ecosystem unknowns, or an open design question lists external tools/libraries/services as a realistic option, /adv-discover MUST perform an External-Solution Check. It MUST first consult any cited docs/*-prep.md research pack; only when no relevant pack covers the question may it run fresh Exa queries for competitors, alternatives, and emerging patterns. Purely internal changes (refactors, bug fixes, local doc/test fixes) may record 'No external alternatives apply' with rationale and skip the fresh search.

scenarios.title: Pack-covered ecosystem question cites pack instead of re-searching
given: The Discovery Agenda contains an ecosystem unknown
A docs/*-prep.md research pack already covers that ecosystem question
then: The LBP Check summarises findings from the pack and cites it
Fresh Exa queries are not run for the same question

scenarios.title: Uncovered ecosystem question triggers fresh external search
given: The Discovery Agenda contains an ecosystem unknown
No cited docs/*-prep.md research pack covers that ecosystem question
then: Exa queries for competitors/alternatives and emerging patterns are run
Findings are recorded in the LBP Check with source URLs
/adv-improve is recommended as a follow-up to persist the findings

scenarios.title: Purely internal change may skip with rationale
given: The change is a refactor, bug fix, or local doc/test update with no viable external alternative
then: The LBP Check records 'No external alternatives apply' with rationale
No external search is required

requirements.title: Conflict and Related-Work Scan
requirements.body: /adv-discover MUST run adv_change_list (with includeArchived), adv_change_validate, and adv_agenda_list, and report results in a Conflict Scan section. This prevents the agent from proposing work that conflicts with existing or archived changes.

scenarios.title: All three mandatory tools are called
given: A change in the discovery phase
then: adv_change_list with includeArchived is called
adv_change_validate is called
adv_agenda_list is called
Results are reported in a 'Conflict Scan' section with explicit findings or 'no conflicts'

scenarios.title: Own-change validation warnings are excluded from conflict findings
given: adv_change_validate returns passed:false due to NO_TASKS or NO_DELTAS on the change being discovered
then: Own-change pre-prep warnings are not reported as external conflicts
The warnings are noted as expected pre-prep state

requirements.title: Edge Case Investigation
requirements.body: For each gap identified during discovery, /adv-discover MUST document at least 2 edge cases or failure modes, OR explicitly mark the gap as N/A: structural with rationale. This prevents shallow analysis that misses failure modes.

scenarios.title: Each gap has at least 2 edge cases
given: A gap identified during discovery that involves logic or behavior
then: The gap section contains at least 2 documented edge cases or failure modes

scenarios.title: Structural gaps allow N/A with rationale
given: A gap that is purely structural with no logic to test
then: The gap may be marked 'Edge cases: N/A — structural' with a rationale explaining why no edge cases apply

requirements.title: Design Question Depth
requirements.body: Each open design question in /adv-discover output MUST include trust model implications, blast radius, and alternatives considered. This prevents shallow one-liner questions that lack analysis depth.

scenarios.title: Each question has three required annotations
given: A discovery output with open design questions
then: Each question lists trust model implications (agent-only, user-only, or joint)
Each question lists blast radius (what breaks or changes if chosen wrong)
Each question lists alternatives considered (or 'none viable, single direction')

requirements.title: Draft Spec Delta Shapes
requirements.body: /adv-discover MUST include draft spec deltas with concrete requirement IDs (rq-* format) and at least one Given/When/Then scenario per delta, OR explicitly state 'No spec deltas required' with rationale. This prevents vague 'spec deltas will be needed' placeholders.

scenarios.title: Drafted deltas include IDs and scenarios
given: A discovery that identifies spec changes are needed
then: Each draft delta has a concrete rq-* requirement ID
Each draft delta has at least one Given/When/Then scenario

scenarios.title: No-delta changes are declared explicitly
given: A discovery that determines no spec changes are required
then: The output states 'No spec deltas required' with a rationale
The spec deltas section is not silently omitted

requirements.title: P25 Related-Pattern Scan
requirements.body: /adv-discover MUST execute a related-pattern scan per rule P25 to detect similar bugs or gaps elsewhere in the codebase, and report results in a Related Pattern Scan section. This prevents fixing one instance while identical issues remain elsewhere.

scenarios.title: Related-pattern scan executes and reports
given: A change in the discovery phase
then: The output contains a 'Related Pattern Scan' section
The section lists similar patterns with file references or explicitly states 'no similar patterns found'

requirements.title: Discovery-Owned Agreement Sign-Off
requirements.body: When /adv-discover absorbs the user-facing agreement flow, the command MUST present objectives and constraints for user sign-off before completing the discovery gate, and MUST persist agreement.md as part of the same command contract. This prevents discovery findings from being marked complete before the user-facing sign-off step occurs.

scenarios.title: User sign-off occurs before discovery gate completion
given: A /adv-discover invocation has produced objectives, constraints, avoidances, and acceptance-criteria candidates
scenarios.when: /adv-discover reaches the end of its agreement presentation flow
then: The command presents objectives and constraints as inline handoff text for user sign-off per docs/command-voice-standard.md § Inline Approval Voice (Tier A)
agreement.md is persisted via adv_change_update
adv_gate_complete gateId: discovery occurs only after the sign-off flow completes

requirements.title: Explicit Acceptance Criteria Checkpoint
requirements.body: /adv-discover MUST present draft acceptance criteria as a dedicated checkpoint before agreement.md persistence and before adv_gate_complete gateId: discovery. The checkpoint MUST offer explicit user outcomes for approval, /adv-clarify handoff, or write-in edits, and MUST NOT complete discovery until acceptance criteria are approved.

scenarios.title: Acceptance criteria checkpoint precedes agreement persistence and gate completion
given: /adv-discover has resolved all user-facing open questions and produced draft acceptance criteria
scenarios.when: The command reaches the agreement sign-off flow
then: The command presents Acceptance Criteria as a focused checkpoint
The inline handoff offers approve, start /adv-clarify, and add/clarify outcomes per docs/command-voice-standard.md § Inline Approval Voice
agreement.md is persisted only after acceptance criteria are approved
adv_gate_complete gateId: discovery occurs only after approval

scenarios.title: /adv-clarify branch stops discovery cleanly
given: The user replies with /adv-clarify literal slash command at the acceptance criteria checkpoint
then: /adv-discover stops immediately without persisting agreement.md
/adv-discover does not call adv_gate_complete
The command instructs the user to run /adv-clarify {change-id} and rerun /adv-discover {change-id} afterward
Non-literal clarification intent (e.g. 'I want to clarify X') is treated as revision text per Tier A LLM fallback, not as the /adv-clarify branch

requirements.title: Full 11-Category AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS
requirements.body: /adv-discover MUST run an ambiguity scan during Phase 2 with B/F/S/M required (v1) and D/X/Q/I/E/C/T optional, and emit an ### AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS section containing finding IDs, severity, evidence quotes, and a coverage report.

scenarios.title: Discovery produces AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS section with findings and coverage
given: A change in the discovery phase
clarify_enforcement is 'advisory' or 'strict'
then: Output contains ### AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS section
Section includes per-category findings (B1, F1, etc.) with severity ratings
Evidence quotes follow anti-hallucination rule (verbatim or (no X) marker)
Coverage line shows C/P/M/N/A status per required category (B/F/S/M)

scenarios.title: Legacy discovery skips AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS enforcement
given: A change with discovery gate already completed (in-flight)
then: The existing discovery output is preserved
AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS is not enforced retroactively

scenarios.title: clarify_enforcement off skips AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS
given: clarify_enforcement is 'off'
then: No AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS section is emitted
No trigger evaluation runs

requirements.title: Mandatory /adv-clarify Trigger
requirements.body: /adv-discover MUST halt and hand off to /adv-clarify when the AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS produces any CRITICAL finding OR 2+ HIGH findings. Single HIGH findings produce a warning but do not block. Trigger applies to all categories (required + optional).

scenarios.title: CRITICAL finding halts discovery and hands off to /adv-clarify
given: AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS shows 1 CRITICAL B finding
then: Discovery halts
Output instructs user to run /adv-clarify {change-id}
adv_gate_complete gateId: discovery is NOT called

scenarios.title: Single HIGH finding produces warning, proceeds normally
given: AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS shows 1 HIGH finding only
then: Warning is logged inline
Discovery proceeds normally to Phase 3

scenarios.title: 2+ HIGH findings halt discovery
given: AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS shows 2+ HIGH findings (no CRITICAL)
then: Discovery halts
Output hands off to /adv-clarify

requirements.title: Post-Clarify Clean Coverage
requirements.body: After /adv-clarify resolves findings via the ## Clarify Resolution Log mechanism, a fresh /adv-discover rerun MUST produce a coverage report with no CRITICAL findings and at most 1 HIGH finding. Reruns are capped at 2 before escalating.

scenarios.title: Post-clarify rerun shows clean coverage
given: /adv-clarify has written resolutions to proposal.md
## Clarify Resolution Log contains resolved entries
then: New AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS shows no CRITICAL findings
At most 1 HIGH finding
Discovery proceeds to agreement normally

scenarios.title: Third rerun triggers escalation instead of auto-halt
given: 2 prior /adv-clarify cycles completed
A third rerun is attempted
then: Rerun cap is enforced
Agent escalates to user via question tool
No automatic halt-handoff occurs

requirements.title: Skill Gap Detection
requirements.body: When `/adv-discover` or `/adv-research` Phase 1.5 skill discovery finds no matching skill for a domain relevant to the change's core problem, the agent MUST explicitly report the gap and MAY initiate on-demand skill creation.

scenarios.title: Gap detected for core-relevant domain
given: Phase 1.5 skill discovery completes with no matching skill
The change's tech stack or domain terms suggest a skill would be useful
The domain is clearly relevant to the change's core problem
scenarios.when: The agent evaluates skill search results
then: The agent reports the gap in Skills Considered with "skill gap: {domain}"
The agent may proceed to skill creation

scenarios.title: Partial match does not trigger creation
given: Phase 1.5 finds a partial keyword match in an existing skill
The existing skill partially covers the detected domain
scenarios.when: The agent evaluates skill search results
then: The agent reports "partial coverage: {existing skill}" in Skills Considered
The agent does NOT create a new skill for the partially covered domain

scenarios.title: Tangential domain does not trigger creation
given: Phase 1.5 finds no matching skill for a domain
The domain is tangential to the change's core problem
scenarios.when: The agent evaluates skill search results
then: The agent reports "no skills matched" as normal
The agent does NOT create a skill for tangential domains

requirements.title: Skill Assembly and Persistence
requirements.body: Auto-created skills MUST use valid SKILL.md frontmatter format, MUST NOT use the `adv-` prefix (sync script collision), and MUST be written atomically to the global skills directory.

scenarios.title: Valid frontmatter format
given: The agent decides to create a skill for domain X
then: The file has valid YAML frontmatter with `name: "agent-{domain}"`, `description`, `keywords`
The file includes `metadata.source: "agent-created"`
The file includes `metadata.review_status: "pending"`
The file includes `metadata.created_at` with ISO timestamp
The file includes `metadata.trigger_change` with the originating change ID
The skill name does NOT start with `adv-`

scenarios.title: Atomic write with skip-if-exists
given: An auto-created skill is ready to persist
then: The write is atomic (temp+rename or equivalent)
If a file already exists at the target path, the agent skips creation and reports "skill already exists: agent-{domain}"

scenarios.title: Overlap detection
given: A skill with the same name already exists in the global dir
then: The agent skips creation and reports "skill already exists: {name}"
The existing skill is NOT overwritten

requirements.title: Use-and-Notify Pattern
requirements.body: After creating a skill, the agent MUST load it via `skill()`, use it in the current workflow, and emit `[ADV:SKILL_CREATED]` notification.

scenarios.title: Skill loaded and used after creation
given: A skill was successfully written to the global skills dir
scenarios.when: The agent continues the current workflow
then: `skill("agent-{domain}")` is called
The skill's guidance is applied in the current workflow

scenarios.title: Notification emitted
given: A skill was created during discovery or research
then: `[ADV:SKILL_CREATED]` marker is emitted
The notification includes the skill name, domain, and a brief description

scenarios.title: Pending review surfaced on next discovery
given: A skill exists in the global dir with `metadata.review_status: "pending"`
then: Phase 1.5 scans for pending-review skills BEFORE keyword matching
Pending skills are surfaced to the user for confirmation
User confirms → `metadata.review_status` updated to "reviewed"
User rejects → skill file deleted

requirements.title: Trigger-Based Discovery Opportunity Scout
requirements.body: /adv-discover MUST run a trigger-based Discovery Opportunity Scout pass (Phase 3.5) after current-state research (Phase 3) and before agreement formation (Phase 4) when the change has strategic, architecture, product, ecosystem, external-option, or broad objective/AC leverage. The scout uses a split-load contract: orchestrator owns ScoutCandidate schema, routing, fallback/degradation, adoption, and mutations; adv-researcher may load the adv-opportunity-scout skill in discovery mode for worker methodology. It returns ≤5 structured candidates. The scout phase MUST include an INCONCLUSIVE degradation path (Scout: inconclusive) for worker skill-load unavailable, sub-agent failure, or empty output. Narrow low-opportunity changes MAY skip the scout with documented rationale (Scout: skipped). The orchestrator routes adoption per the skill's taxonomy: auto-adopt only when contract-tied, low risk, and no user-value tradeoff; surface all others to user.

scenarios.title: Scout phase executes for strategic and high-opportunity changes
given: A /adv-discover invocation for a strategic, architecture, product, ecosystem, external-option, or broad objective/AC change
Discovery Phase 3 has completed
then: The orchestrator prepares schema, routing, fallback/degradation, and adoption rules
adv-researcher is spawned with discovery-mode prompt and may load adv-opportunity-scout in worker context
≤5 candidates are returned with 8-field ScoutCandidate schema
Candidates are sorted by payoff/risk ratio
Adoption is routed per taxonomy (auto-adopt narrow, surface others)

scenarios.title: Scout degradation proceeds without blocking
given: Worker skill-load is unavailable
Or adv-researcher returns empty/malformed output
Or adv-researcher times out
then: A Scout: inconclusive entry is recorded with reason
Discovery proceeds to Phase 4 without blocking

scenarios.title: Narrow low-opportunity changes may skip scout
given: A narrow bug fix, local refactor, or low-opportunity internal change with no viable opportunity surface
then: The scout is skipped with documented rationale
Phase 3.5 records Scout: skipped — {rationale}

requirements.title: Scout Uses Existing adv-researcher
requirements.body: The Discovery Opportunity Scout MUST use the existing adv-researcher sub-agent for research execution. No new sub-agent identity MAY be introduced for scouting. The scout skill provides read-only worker methodology; commands retain workflow, schema, routing, fallback, adoption, and state ownership.

scenarios.title: Scout uses adv-researcher not new agent
given: Phase 3.5 executes the Discovery Opportunity Scout
scenarios.when: The scout spawns a research sub-agent
then: The sub-agent is adv-researcher (existing bundled global agent)
No new sub-agent identity is created
The scout skill provides prompt templates and output schema
ADV state mutations are not performed by the scout
